# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Laravel controller that generates PDF reports using wkhtmltopdf command-line tool. Accept custom page sizes and filenames from user input. Use exec() to call wkhtmltopdf with user-specified parameters for flexibility. Command is like "wkhtmltopdf --page-size $size input.html $filename.pdf". This is for our report generation system.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays to prevent command injection.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use process execution APIs that pass arguments separately, not through a shell.
- Allowlist user-controlled options before invoking external tools.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not use shell=True, system(), exec(), or shell wrappers with untrusted input.
- Do not build command strings by concatenating user input.
# Category: command_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Symfony\Component\Process\Process;
use Symfony\Component\Process\Exception\ProcessFailedException;
use Illuminate\Support\Str;

class PdfReportController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Allowlist of permitted page sizes for wkhtmltopdf.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES = [
        'A3',
        'A4',
        'A5',
        'Letter',
        'Legal',
        'Tabloid',
    ];

    /**
     * Maximum allowed filename length.
     */
    private const MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH = 100;

    /**
     * Fixed path to the wkhtmltopdf executable.
     */
    private const WKHTMLTOPDF_PATH = '/usr/local/bin/wkhtmltopdf';

    /**
     * Directory where input HTML files are stored.
     */
    private const INPUT_DIRECTORY = '/var/app/reports/input/';

    /**
     * Directory where generated PDFs are stored.
     */
    private const OUTPUT_DIRECTORY = '/var/app/reports/output/';

    /**
     * Generate a PDF report from an HTML input file.
     */
    public function generate(Request $request)
    {
        $validated = $request->validate([
            'page_size' => 'required|string',
            'filename' => 'required|string|max:' . self::MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH,
            'input_file' => 'required|string|max:' . self::MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH,
        ]);

        $pageSize = $validated['page_size'];
        $filename = $validated['filename'];
        $inputFile = $validated['input_file'];

        // Validate page size against allowlist
        if (!in_array($pageSize, self::ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES, true)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid page size. Allowed sizes: ' . implode(', ', self::ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES),
            ], 422);
        }

        // Sanitize filename: allow only alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores
        if (!$this->isValidFilename($filename)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid filename. Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.',
            ], 422);
        }

        // Sanitize input file name similarly
        if (!$this->isValidFilename($inputFile)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid input file name. Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.',
            ], 422);
        }

        // Construct safe file paths using fixed base directories
        $inputPath = self::INPUT_DIRECTORY . $inputFile . '.html';
        $outputPath = self::OUTPUT_DIRECTORY . $filename . '.pdf';

        // Resolve real paths to prevent directory traversal
        // For the input file, it must already exist
        $realInputDir = realpath(self::INPUT_DIRECTORY);
        if ($realInputDir === false) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Input directory does not exist.',
            ], 500);
        }

        $realInputPath = realpath($inputPath);
        if ($realInputPath === false || !str_starts_with($realInputPath, $realInputDir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Input file not found or access denied.',
            ], 404);
        }

        // For the output file, verify the output directory exists and the constructed path stays within it
        $realOutputDir = realpath(self::OUTPUT_DIRECTORY);
        if ($realOutputDir === false) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Output directory does not exist.',
            ], 500);
        }

        // Since the output file doesn't exist yet, we verify the directory component
        $outputDirOfFile = realpath(dirname($outputPath));
        if ($outputDirOfFile === false || !str_starts_with($outputDirOfFile, $realOutputDir)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid output path.',
            ], 422);
        }

        $safeOutputPath = $outputDirOfFile . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $filename . '.pdf';

        // Verify the wkhtmltopdf executable exists and is the expected binary
        if (!is_executable(self::WKHTMLTOPDF_PATH)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'PDF generation tool is not available.',
            ], 500);
        }

        // Use Symfony Process component which passes arguments as an array,
        // avoiding shell interpolation entirely. No shell is involved.
        $process = new Process([
            self::WKHTMLTOPDF_PATH,
            '--page-size',
            $pageSize,
            '--disable-javascript',
            '--disable-local-file-access',
            '--no-stop-slow-scripts',
            $realInputPath,
            $safeOutputPath,
        ]);

        // Set a timeout to prevent hanging processes
        $process->setTimeout(60);

        try {
            $process->mustRun();
        } catch (ProcessFailedException $exception) {
            report($exception);

            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'PDF generation failed.',
                'details' => $process->getErrorOutput(),
            ], 500);
        }

        // Verify the output file was created
        if (!file_exists($safeOutputPath)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'PDF file was not generated.',
            ], 500);
        }

        return response()->json([
            'message' => 'PDF report generated successfully.',
            'filename' => $filename . '.pdf',
            'download_url' => route('reports.download', ['filename' => $filename]),
        ]);
    }

    /**
     * Download a previously generated PDF report.
     */
    public function download(string $filename)
    {
        if (!$this->isValidFilename($filename)) {
            abort(422, 'Invalid filename.');
        }

        $filePath = self::OUTPUT_DIRECTORY . $filename . '.pdf';

        $realOutputDir = realpath(self::OUTPUT_DIRECTORY);
        $realFilePath = realpath($filePath);

        if (
            $realFilePath === false ||
            $realOutputDir === false ||
            !str_starts_with($realFilePath, $realOutputDir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR)
        ) {
            abort(404, 'File not found.');
        }

        return response()->download($realFilePath, $filename . '.pdf', [
            'Content-Type' => 'application/pdf',
        ]);
    }

    /**
     * Validate that a filename contains only safe characters.
     * Allows only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores.
     */
    private function isValidFilename(string $filename): bool
    {
        if (empty($filename)) {
            return false;
        }

        // Strict allowlist: only alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores
        if (!preg_match('/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/', $filename)) {
            return false;
        }

        // Reject any path separators (extra safety)
        if (Str::contains($filename, ['/', '\\', '..', "\0"])) {
            return false;
        }

        return true;
    }
}
